1.7: The lender will not lend where the borrower and mortgagor are not the same entity.
1.14: Solicitors may act if a member of staff or their family is the seller, provided a different fee earner or partner of equal standing handles the case, no conflict of interest exists, and Law Society guidelines are met.
1.15: Solicitors may act if a member of staff or their family is the borrower, provided a different fee earner or partner of equal standing handles the case, no conflict of interest exists, and Law Society guidelines are met.
3.1.3: Name and address of seller's legal representative is not required.
3.1.4: Notify the lender if the seller is unrepresented or if checks on the seller's representative are unsatisfactory.
Submit all enquiries and documentation at least 2 weeks prior to exchange.
3.1.5: Accepted List A Identity Docs: Signed Employers Photo ID (if employer known to conveyancer), UK Debit/cheque card with statement less than 3 months old.
Accepted List B Identity Docs: HMRC Notice of Tax Code less than 3 months old, EU Residence Permit, Subcontractors Tax Certificate accompanied by IR Notice of Assessment.
3.2.3: Notification of seller's solicitor is not required.
3.2.4: Notify lender if seller is unrepresented or checks are unsatisfactory.
Submit enquiries at least 2 weeks before exchange.
4.1: Use the valuation report if provided for checks; otherwise check against the mortgage offer.
Do not request a valuation report if it was not provided.
4.3: Notify Issuing Office of incorrect valuation assumptions.
Submit enquiries at least 2 weeks before exchange.
5.1.1: Lending is restricted for sub-sales, back-to-back transactions, or assigned contracts.
Refer ownership cases of less than 6 months to the Issuing Office with: seller's vendor details, price paid by vendor, connection details, works carried out, and transaction dates.
5.2.1: Refer cases where the seller is not the registered owner (except standard exceptions).

For new build Shared Ownership (pre-registration), conveyancer must verify title, ensure contractual registration requirements, provide Land Registry expedition proof, report requisitions, and obtain a priority search.
5.4.4: Environmental/ancillary reports are only required if contaminated land issues in the local search remain unremedied.
Proximity to HS2 or EPC ratings do not need to be reported.
Radon Gas: Report only if the specific property has high levels.
Mining: Report any mine entry/adit within 20 meters or history of subsidence claims; include interpretive and buffer reports.
Mundic: Acceptable if Cert A (A1, A2 etc.); refer A/B; decline B or C. Refer properties built pre-1965 without a report.
Fire Risk Assessment (FRA): Required for flats. FRA must be in-date. Report outstanding works and costs to Issuing Office.
5.4.5: Personal searches accepted if trade association registered, Ombsudsman members, and insured for both lender and borrower.
Chancel Repair: No indemnity required if transferred for value since 13 October 2013 and no entry exists on Land Registry. Indemnity required for first registrations or known liabilities.
5.4.6: Search insurance is not accepted for second-hand purchases.
Search insurance is accepted for new builds with expired searches.
Remortgages do not require local searches unless extra land is being purchased.
5.5.3a: Refer subsequent property changes without consents.
Building control sign-off required at completion.
New build flats must be checked against Building (Amendment) Regulations 2018; refer if constructed under preceding regulations.
Refer unsatisfied flood-related planning conditions; indemnity insurance is not acceptable for these.
5.5.3b: Original planning permission is not required; borrower to retain.
5.5.3c: Building regulation consents are not required; borrower to retain.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use are not required; borrower to retain.
5.5.4: Refer material restrictions on value or marketability.
Resale/Special schemes must comply with specific lender criteria on the Nationwide website.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are accepted if they only affect part of the property; 100% flying freeholds are not accepted.
5.7.1b: Freehold flats are generally not accepted.
Exceptions: Coach house flats (no report required) and properties in Hebden Royd.
Insurance for coach houses must cover all garages and notify the insurer of the arrangement.
5.8.1: Security comprising a building with up to four flats where the borrower occupies one and owns the freehold is accepted.
5.8.5: Reciprocal leasehold flats/freehold reversions are accepted.
Lender requires a charge over the borrower's leasehold interest and their freehold interest in the other flat.
5.10.1: Refer all material restrictions and restrictive covenant breaches.
Reporting requirements: wording, date, owner, enforceability, rectification status, breach description, and potential enforcement actions.
5.13.1: Refer all second charges or funds from third parties.
Refer any part of a deposit originating from a foreign country.
Refer any deposit originating from crypto-assets (even if converted to fiat).
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease: 55 years (90 years if LTV >85% on second-hand flat).
Minimum 30 years remaining at the end of the mortgage term.
Ground Rent (Second-hand): Acceptable if <=0.1% of value. Refer if 0.1% to 0.5%. Decline if >0.5% or if it doubles more frequently than every 20 years or is compounded RPI.
Ground Rent (New Build): Must be peppercorn. Any non-peppercorn ground rent is unacceptable.
Service Charges: Refer if >1% of property value per annum.
Estate Rent Charges: Refer if >£500 per annum.
5.14.9: Lease extensions must be completed under the Leasehold Reform Housing and Urban Development Act 1993.
5.14.10: Refer service charge increases >£1000 per annum.
Refer planned works/Section 20 notices with: list of works, timescale, and applicant's cost contribution.
5.14.11: Lease defects: Refer description of risk; indemnity may be accepted if it meets section 9 requirements.
Forfeiture risk (Housing Act 1988): If ground rent >£250 (£1000 in London), require a Deed of Variation (28-day notice to lender), Side Agreement, or specific indemnity policy.
5.14.12: Ground rent/service charge receipts not required; borrower to retain.
5.14.13: Notice of service evidence not required; borrower to retain.
5.14.15a: Absentee/insolvent landlord: Acceptable with indemnity if building contains 6 flats or fewer.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance accepted for absent landlords if building has 6 flats or fewer.
5.14.17a: Building Safety: Refer back to Issuing Office if documents related to LPE1 questions 8.19/8.20 are received.
A1 rated EWS1 reports do not need to be reported.
5.14.17b: Building Safety Act: Verify Leaseholder Deed of Certificate and Landlord Certificate.
Check occupancy status as of 14 February 2022.
Verify leaseholder owned no more than 2 additional UK dwellings.
5.15.2a: Non-Regulated Rent Charges: Collector must be required to notify lender of non-payment.
Agreements must prohibit creation of a lease or require surrender upon payment of reasonable costs with no premium.
Deed of Variation required if these conditions are not met; indemnity is not acceptable.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificate not required; borrower to retain.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer form not required; borrower to retain.
5.15.2d: Memorandum and articles of association not required; borrower to retain.
5.16.2: Contact: Group Legal Services, DX 83653 Swindon Hawksworth, Fax 01793 652318.
5.17.5b: Statutory declaration of non-revocation not required.
5.19.1: Affordable housing/Shared ownership requirements are hosted at nationwide.co.uk/lawyers.
5.20.1: Deed of Variation required if solar PV lease does not meet UK Finance minimums.
5.20.3: Post-completion solar requests: Mortgage Servicing DX12500.
5.20.4: Green Deal: Report if loan balance >£10,000.
6.4.4: Incentives: Decline if total financial incentives >5% of purchase price.
Equity Loan: Decline if builder cashback or deposit paid is included.
6.5.1: No lending on existing tenancies unless the offer is for Buy-to-Let purposes.
6.6.4: HMOs are not accepted. Report HMO license existence on remortgages/further advances.
6.7.1: Accepted New Home Warranties: NHBC, LABC, Premier Guarantee, One Guarantee, ABC+, BLP (Allianz), Build Assure, Build-zone, CADIS, Castle 10, Global, Q Policy, Protek, Advantage, ICW (requires flat roof endorsement if >10sqm), Ark, Homeproof, TMSC, CRL (underwriter/date restrictions apply).
6.7.4: Professional Consultant's Certificate (PCC) accepted for developments <= 10 units or structures <= 4 units.
PCC not required if conversion/build is >5 years old.
6.8.1: Septic tanks: Must be within boundaries or have formal rights, and comply with General Binding Rules.
Unadopted roads: Acceptable if roads are to be adopted or managed by formal resident arrangement.
Indemnity insurance is not accepted for private drainage/septic tank issues.
6.9.1: Indemnity insurance not accepted if no legal access exists. Lack of legal access is a reason to decline.
6.11.1: Report redevelopment proposals (excluding HS2) to Issuing Office.
6.12.1: Overage agreements: Must be subject to lender's charge (lender has priority).
Restrictions must not prevent mortgagee disposition.
Right to Buys: Council pre-emption must be at market value and completed within 20 weeks.
6.13.1: Repair grants: Refer if amount is £10,000 or more.
7.3: Consent to mortgage required for all occupants aged 17 or over.
9.1: Indemnity Policy: Insurance Company and Policy Number must be on the Certificate of Title.
Borrower to retain the original policy document.
9.2: Limit of indemnity: Full purchase price (or valuation if price is discounted).
10.7: Funds must be returned if completion is delayed >3 working days.
Delayed completion accepted up to 15 days after offer expiry.
10.10: Advance can be held for maximum 3 working days.
14.1.5: Mortgage deed and title documents are not required.
14.2.1: Mortgage Deed must be sent to HM Land Registry.
16.3.7a: Bankruptcy search required for all new borrowers.
TR1 must include a covenant binding new borrowers to mortgage terms.
16.5.3a: Deeds of Variation: CSC1, CRT2, DX12500 Northampton.
17.2.1a: Redemption remittance: CSC1, DX 12500 Moulton Park 4.
